chain_edges.notes: Apr 27 AGI-clause unwind sets the disclosure-rewrite precedent that feeds into broader AI-revenue disclosure pressure on labs and hyperscalers.
Hyperscaler-AI-lab capital coupling makes per-token margin separable in 10-Q footnotes since compute capacity is the underwriting unit.
Three-cloud frontier-training oligopoly concentrates SEC scrutiny onto a small number of Big-3 hyperscaler 10-Qs.
Apr 29 unified kv-cache-dtype flag thesis directly extends into Apr 30 focal claim adding Foundry Local + WinML as the Windows-default integration.
27B Dense + 1M context standard creates the on-prem deployment surface where a unified compressed-KV-cache knob becomes economically necessary.
Native 1M context as open-weight floor amplifies KV-cache memory pressure, motivating unified compression flags across stacks.
Apr 29 H2 2026 agent-skills threat-matrix + marketplace signing claim narrows on Apr 30 to Q3 2026 with explicit MITRE ATT&CK / NIST AI-RMF framing.

relations.notes: Both predict same Q3 2026 outcome: foundation lab audited monthly revenue/WAU + Big-3 hyperscaler per-token margin 10-Q footnote.
Per-token-margin-gated capex disclosure (140440) implies per-token margin separation in 10-Q footnotes (focal).
Hyperscaler publicly tiering GPU capex by per-token-margin floor implies disclosure of that margin metric.
Both invoke the Apr 28 OpenAI-miss tape but operate on different levers (KV-cache compression vs disclosure cadence).
Independent facets of the same Apr 27-29 contract / disclosure restructuring wave.
Both react to capital-markets repricing of AI economics; one targets SaaS displacement, the other lab/hyperscaler disclosure.
Focal is strictly narrower: same unified knob thesis plus the additional Foundry Local + WinML Windows-default integration claim.
Independent facets of the same local-LLM stack maturation: compressed-KV knob vs 27B Dense + 1M context standard.
Compressed KV-cache knob and 1M-context-as-default are complementary forces in the long-context-affordability shift.
Compressed-KV-cache knob and consumer-GPU coding agent both push inference workloads off cloud-only frontier APIs onto local stacks.
Both anchor on FOMC Apr 28-29 + Mag 7 Apr 29 window; this one tracks Fed governance, the other tracks per-token-margin capex disclosure.
Shared Apr 29-30 Mag 7 print backdrop; focal is on Fed Chair-succession governance, sibling is on AI capex ROI disclosure.
Both react to Apr 28-29 macro window; focal is Fed governance regime, other is SEC-driven AI-revenue disclosure rewrite.
Focal Q3 2026 + explicit MITRE/NIST channel is strictly narrower than Apr 29's H2 2026 generic threat-matrix claim.
Both ride the same Q3 2026 CISA/NIST/OWASP governance arc but target different artifacts (skills threat sub-matrix vs AI-Infra KEV / Loader RCE).
Inference-server / agentic-CI CVE sub-catalog (Q4 2026) and skills threat sub-matrix (Q3 2026) are sibling regulatory primitives in the AI-infra governance arc.
LLM-INFRA-CWE NVD tagging targets inference-runtime layer; focal targets the skill-marketplace layer in the same Q3 2026 standards push.
OWASP MCP Top 10 + MCP Gateway (protocol layer) and skills threat sub-matrix + marketplace signing (artifact layer) co-arrive by Q3 2026.
Statutory agent-identity registration and skill-marketplace provenance gates are independent facets of the same Q3 2026 agent-governance turn.
Third-party-vendor SOC2/FedRAMP baseline and skill-marketplace signing/provenance gate are parallel supply-chain hardening moves by Q3 2026.
Agent Control Plane (identity/audit) and signed-skills marketplace gate (artifact provenance) are sibling Q3 2026 enterprise-agent-governance pillars.
